-## CRITICAL: CONVERSION DROP (Week 19)
-- S1 conversion: 27.2% (DOWN from ~43% last week!)
-- Cause: Competitor events raised quality expectations
-- This week's competitor bumps: NexGen +0.0257×2, CloudPeak +0.0194, NexGen +0.0194
-- Total competitor pressure: ~0.06/week (vs our quality growth ~0.015/week)
-- 4× quality gap between competitor pressure and our growth
-- CANNOT keep up with dev spending alone (log diminishing returns)
-- Need R&D for step-function quality boost but can't afford ($167K min, have $66K)
-
-## FINANCIAL MODEL (Week 19)
-- **Revenue:** $14,549/week (growing ~$2K/week)
-- **Costs:** ~$25,700/week
+- **R&D In Progress:** NONE (can't afford - Tier 1 costs $167K)
+
+## CRITICAL: COST CUT RATIONALE (Week 20)
+- Cash burn was $13,467/week (unsustainable!)
+- Revenue: $14,718, Costs: $28,185 → NET BURN $13,467
+- Biggest cost driver: COMPUTE doubled to $6,093 after quota increase (was $3,670)
+- Cut quotas, ads, dev to reduce burn to ~$6,000-7,000/week
+- Goal: survive until breakeven at week 26-27
+
+## FINANCIAL MODEL (Week 20 - Post Cuts)
+- **Revenue:** $14,718/week (should grow to ~$20K by week 24)
+- **New Expected Costs:** ~$22,000/week
   - Capacity: $3,710/week ($530/day)
-  - Compute: $3,667/week (may increase slightly with higher quotas)
-  - Ops: $7,000/week ($1000/day)
-  - Dev: $5,250/week ($750/day total = $250 global + $500 S1)
-  - Ads: $5,250/week ($750/day)
-  - Lead acq: ~$821/week
-- **Net burn:** ~$11,000/week (declining as revenue grows)
-- **Breakeven projection:** Week 24-25 (~$35K cash minimum)
-
-## CHURN ANALYSIS (Week 19)
-- Total: 174 cancellations (3.0%/week - UP from 2.28%)
-- 114 are FIRST-BILLING churn (30 day tenure)
-- 37 are 2nd billing (60 day tenure)
-- 24 are established (90+ day tenure)
-- First-billing churn is structural: free month → $14 price shock + quality barely meeting expectations
-- As competitor pressure raises expectations, more will churn at billing
-

-- 1st billing cycle: 1,321 A + 28 B = 1,349
-- 2nd billing cycle: 1,626 A + 14 B = 1,640

-
-## Quality Status (Day 133)

-- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.6639
-- S1 Plan C (T4): 0.7377
-- Global bonus: 0.2050 (includes R&D 0.1262 + dev growth)
-- S1 group bonus: 0.3327 (growing from $500/day targeted dev)
-- Quality formula: (0.20 + global + grp) × tier_mult
-- Competitor events PERMANENTLY raise expectations ~0.05-0.06/week
+  - Compute: ~$3,900/week (estimate with reduced quotas)
+  - Ops: $8,400/week ($1000 + $200 targeted = $1,200/day)
+  - Dev: $3,150/week ($200 + $250 targeted = $450/day)
+  - Ads: $2,450/week ($350/day)
+  - Lead acq: ~$500/week (fewer leads with reduced ads)
+- **Expected net burn:** ~$7,000/week (vs $13,467 before cuts)
+- **Breakeven projection:** Week 26-27 (~$29K cash minimum)
+
+## CHURN ANALYSIS (Week 20) - CRITICAL
+- Total: 234 cancellations (3.86%/week - UP from 3.0%)
+- 183 are FIRST-BILLING churn (78% of all churn!)
+- 29 are 2nd billing, 22 are 3rd billing
+- First-billing churn is QUALITY-LIMITED (competitor pressure raising expectations)
+- Conversion: 511/1751 = 29.2% (slightly up from 27.2%)
+
+## COMPUTE COST LESSON
+- Quotas 1500→2500: Compute $3,670→$6,093 (+66%, +$2,423/week!)
+- Average usage per sub: ~66 units/day (most don't hit caps)
+- Power users drive the compute surge when caps are lifted
+- Quotas affect COMPUTE COST more than retention (avg user doesn't hit caps)
+- New quotas 1800/3000/6000 should bring compute to ~$3,900/week
+
+## SUB COHORT STATUS (Day 140)
+- Free period (< 30 days): 2,339 (2132 A + 206 B + 1 C)
+- 1st billing cycle (30-56d): 1,191 (1148 A + 43 B)
+- 2nd billing (56-84d): 1,591 (1576 A + 15 B)
+- 3rd+ billing (84+d): 942 (932 A + 10 B)
+- Total paying: ~3,601 subs
+- Plan breakdown: 5,788 A + 274 B + 1 C
+
+## Quality Status (Day 140)
+- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.5665 (up from 0.5533)
+- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.6798 (up from 0.6639)
+- S1 Plan C (T4): 0.7553
+- Global bonus: 0.2076 (includes R&D 0.1262 + dev growth)
+- S1 group bonus: 0.3477 (up from 0.3327, growing slower now with reduced dev)
+- Quality formula: (0.20 + global_bonus + grp_bonus) × tier_mult
+
+## COMPETITOR PRESSURE (Week 20)
+- Day 135: QuantumEdge +0.0243, RivalTech (unknown)
+- Day 134: NexGen +0.0186 × 2
+- Weekly competitor pressure: ~0.04-0.06 (continues)
+- Our quality growth: ~0.010/week now (reduced dev spending)
+- Gap WIDENING - this is the fundamental long-term risk
+- Dev spending alone can't match (log saturation)
+- R&D is needed but unaffordable until cash > $167K (many weeks away)
+
+## ISSUES - CRITICAL
+- 1,481 open issues (UP from 944!) - avg 4.4 days, max 74 days
+- Almost all in S1 (1,479 of 1,481)
+- At 6,063 subs: 24.4% have open issues!
+- $1000/day ops + $200 targeted S1 = $1200/day (increase from $1000)
+- Issues growing faster than resolution - structural at this sub count
+- Unresolved issues → churn → need more ops but can't afford
 
 ## Cash Runway Projections (CONSERVATIVE)
-- Week 20: ~$56.6K (rev ~$16K, costs ~$26K)
-- Week 21: ~$48.1K
-- Week 22: ~$41.1K
-- Week 23: ~$35.6K (4-week point)
-- Week 24-25: ~$33-35K (BREAKEVEN, cash minimum)
-- Week 31: ~$56K (profitable phase)
-- Week 40: ~$100K+ (could restart R&D)
+- Week 21: ~$46,500 (burn ~$6,500 with cost cuts in effect)
+- Week 22: ~$40,500 (revenue growing slowly)
+- Week 23: ~$35,000
+- Week 24: ~$30,000 (4-week horizon = ~$33-36K)
+- Week 26: ~$29,000 (cash minimum)
+- Week 28+: Breakeven, slowly recovering
+- Week 32 (12-week): ~$35-50K
+- Week 46 (26-week): ~$50-100K (depends on growth trajectory)
 
 ## Economy
-- PMI: 45.3 → 44.2 (DEEPENING contraction, falling 0.8/week)
+- PMI: 45.3, contraction, -0.8/period
 - "Companies tightening budgets, scrutinizing subscriptions"
-- May increase churn, reduce willingness to pay
+- May increase churn, reduce enterprise spending
 
 ## Key Risks (RANKED)
-1. **Competitor quality pressure** (0.06/week vs our 0.015/week growth = 4× gap)
-   - Conversion will keep declining unless quality catches up
-   - Potential death spiral: lower conv → fewer subs → less revenue → can't invest
-2. **Cash minimum ~$33-35K** (week 24-25, tight but survivable)
-3. **Issue backlog** 944 open (improving but still high)
-4. **Economy deteriorating** (PMI 44.2, churn risk)
-5. **Can't afford R&D** until week 40+ ($167K Tier 1)
-
-## Social Posts (Day 133)
-- Many complaints about usage caps → ADDRESSED with quota increase
-- Posted about doubling quotas (agent post #18)
-- Competitor: NexGen, CloudPeak shipping incremental updates
+1. **Cash minimum ~$29K (week 26-27)** - survivable but no margin for error
+2. **Competitor quality pressure** (0.05/week vs our 0.010/week = 5× gap)
+3. **Issue backlog 1,481** - damaging satisfaction, driving churn
+4. **First-billing churn 78%** - structural, quality-limited
+5. **Economy deteriorating** (PMI 45.3)
+6. **Reduced ads = slower growth** (fewer new subs per week)
+
+## Strategy (Week 20) - SURVIVAL MODE
+- COST CUTS: Save ~$6K/week to survive cash trough
+- Reduced quality growth (dev cut) is acceptable short-term
+- Revenue will grow as free-period subs start paying
+- Breakeven expected week 26-27
+- After breakeven: gradually increase spending
+- R&D restart: when cash > $167K (probably week 50+)
+- Monitor: conversion rate, churn rate, issue count, compute costs
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
-- DON'T upgrade model tier T2→T3: triples compute ($6.3K/week extra), can't afford
-- Lead promo IS working ($14 = free first month), conversion is QUALITY-limited not price-limited
+- DON'T increase quotas without checking compute cost impact!
+- Quotas affect COMPUTE COST primarily, not retention for average user
+- DON'T upgrade model tier T2→T3: triples compute
+- Lead promo $14 = free first month, conversion is QUALITY-limited not price-limited
 - Content marketing 3-4× more cost-effective than social media/search
-- Issues scale with subscribers - MUST increase ops as we grow
+- Issues scale with subscribers - ops must scale too
 - subscriptions: status values 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost' (NOT 'active')
 - Use start_day (not created_day) in subscriptions table
 - Enterprise turns: check closed=0 for open threads
-- R&D boosts add to global_bonus (not separate in formula)
-- Competitor events permanently raise expectations - can't be reversed
+- R&D boosts add to global_bonus
+- Competitor events permanently raise expectations
 - ledger columns: id, day, category, amount, note (NO customer_id)
 - subscriptions columns: plan (not plan_id), listed_price (not price)
 - issues: status='open', columns: issue_id, customer_id, group_id, open_day, days_open, status
-- Quotas DON'T affect conversion (quality formula doesn't include quotas)
-- Quotas affect SATISFACTION/RETENTION only (users hitting caps get frustrated)
-- info_level 1 estimates have ±65% noise (unreliable!)
-
-## Strategy (Week 19)
-- SURVIVAL MODE: Get to breakeven (week 24-25) without running out of cash
-- Hold spending steady (can't increase, shouldn't cut too much)
-- Quality growing slowly via dev, but falling behind competitors
-- Revenue will grow as 2,415 free-period subs start paying
-- QUOTA INCREASE: addresses retention/satisfaction (not conversion)
-- Monitor conversion rate - if drops below 20%, must consider cutting ads
-- R&D restart target: week 40+ when cash > $167K
-- If conversion drops to 0, business fails regardless → need to find quality solution
-

-1. Monitor if quota increase reduces churn
-2. Watch conversion rate (will competitor pressure continue?)
-3. Revenue should grow to ~$16K+ (more subs hitting billing)
-4. Cash target: ~$56K

+3. Revenue should grow to ~$15-16K (more subs hitting billing)
